reps:
Here are a few evocative opening lines, depending on the tone you wish to set:

**Option 1 (Atmospheric & Melancholic)**
> "The horizon had long since surrendered to the gray, leaving only the rhythm of the waves and the salt stinging my eyes
The horizon did not end; it simply forgot where the sky left off and the sea began.
The ocean did not crash against the hull; it simply inhaled, pulling the ship’s shadow deeper into the abyss with a silence so heavy it felt like the end of the world.
"The sea did not roar; it whispered the names of those it had already swallowed."

Here are a few options, depending on the tone you wish to set:

**Atmospheric & Moody:**
> "The salt had long since stopped tasting like water and begun to feel like the air itself."
